title: 5 things a man of God builds in private
caption:
Nobody gives a man credit for his 6 AM when he's the only one who knows about it.

That's the point.

The men who are actually worth knowing — the ones who lead well, who can be counted on, who you'd want in a foxhole — they all built something in private first. Before the position, before the respect, before anyone asked. Private repetition is the whole thing.

Five things a man of God builds when nobody's watching:

1. The private habit — prayer, Scripture, the run before it was worth posting.
2. The ability to sit still — with discomfort, loneliness, the itch he can't name. He stops filling it and starts facing it.
3. A word that holds — in the small things, before anyone cared if he kept it.
4. A verse that has cost him something — not recited, lived. The one he stood on when nothing else held.
5. One brother — one man who has his number, knows his sin, and shows up at 10 PM.

"Whatever you do, work at it with all your heart, as working for the Lord, not for human masters." — Colossians 3:23.

This is your 20s. The build happens now or it doesn't happen.

—

Comment "private" and I'll send you the link to ETHOS.
